'''Acorvus''', also known as '''Lazerian III''', was the third [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Lazerian system/Legends|Lazerian system]], [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] the [[Color/Legends|yellow]] [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Lazerian (star)|Lazerian]].<ref name="TSoK" /> It was located within the [[Kira sector/Legends|Kira sector]],<ref name="TEA-OC" /> in the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="TEA" /> Along with the rest of the Lazerian [[Star system/Legends|system]], Acorvus lay on the [[Harrin Trade Corridor]] and [[Kira Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]]s.<ref name="TSoK" />

The planet's surface featured a barren [[Desert/Legends|desert]], and the world had no [[Moon/Legends|moons]]. It had a Type II [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]],<ref name="TSoK" /> in which the use of [[Breath mask/Legends|breath masks]] was suggested.<ref name="Planets">''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume One]]''</ref> Acorvus was the closest neighbor to the planet [[Lazerian IV]], and at one point it was surveyed by the [[Lazerian (people)|inhabitants]] of that world. Eventually Acorvus came to serve as a [[Prison/Legends|penal]] [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] and [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] planet for [[Politics/Legends|political]] dissenters and criminals. The planet was off-limits to all non-authorized personnel.<ref name="TSoK" />

==Behind the scenes==
Acorvus was created by [[John Terra]] and introduced in [[Twin Stars of Kira (sourcebook)|''Twin Stars of Kira'']], a [[1993]] supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. In the book, the planet's name was misspelled as "Acoruus" on one instance.<ref name="TSoK" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Lazerian system, and therefore Acorvus, in grid square N-15.<ref name="TEA" />
